name: Heritage Bank Of St Tammany
description_short: Heritage Bank of St. Tammany is a Louisiana-based community bank operating since 1924, now merged with OnPath Credit Union to offer expanded branch access and digital banking services.
description_long: Heritage Bank of St. Tammany has served the Louisiana community since 1924 as a local community bank focused on helping customers achieve their financial goals. In a significant structural change, Heritage Bank merged with OnPath Credit Union, combining the bank's deep local roots in St. Tammany Parish with OnPath's expanded 17-branch network across the region. This merger was framed as bringing customers "the best of both worlds: the local service you love, plus the power of an expanded 17-branch network and enhanced mobile banking." The institution offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including multiple checking and savings account options, certificates of deposit, money market accounts, credit cards (ranging from low-rate to World Elite), consumer and business loans, vehicle financing (auto, recreational, marine), home mortgages, home equity lines of credit, and investment services including IRAs. Digital banking services include online banking, mobile app access, bill pay, Zelle transfers, and card control features. The company also provides financial counseling and skip-a-pay options for members facing temporary hardship. What distinguishes Heritage Bank/OnPath is its positioning as a people-first financial institution with explicit community commitment, including a dedicated OnPath Foundation focused on education, homeownership, and entrepreneurship in Southeast Louisiana. The merger specifically emphasizes maintaining local service while gaining operational scale. Physical locations remain in Covington, Madisonville, Slidell, and at Copperstill Marketplace, with direct phone contact available at each branch. As a merged entity now operating under OnPath Credit Union branding, Heritage Bank serves traditional banking customers seeking local community connection with modern digital capabilities. However, the transition from independent bank to credit union subsidiary represents a structural change that may affect some legacy customer relationships or service availability. The company's focus remains on personal and small business banking rather than specialized lending or investment management.
services: Personal checking accounts (standard, rewards high-yield, rewards cash back, forward, and rise checking options)
Business checking and money market accounts
Savings accounts including Christmas Club and money market options
Personal and business credit cards with multiple tiers
Consumer loans (signature loans, lines of credit, tuition loans)
Auto loans for new, used, and recreational vehicles
Mortgage products including conventional, FHA/VA, home equity, HELOC, and construction loans
Online banking and mobile banking with bill pay and Zelle transfers
Card Control, ITM access, and overdraft protection services
Auto buying center with preferred dealer network and insurance products
pros: Established community bank with 100-year history since 1924, providing continuity and local relationship banking
Expanded to 17-branch network through OnPath Credit Union merger, offering greater access than standalone operation
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, credit cards, personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, and investment services
Mobile banking app available for iPhone, iPad, and Android with online banking and bill pay capabilities
OnPath Foundation provides community support focused on education, homeownership, and entrepreneurship in Southeast Louisiana
Multiple account options with specialized products like Rewards High Yield Checking, Business Money Market, and Physician/Professional Home Loans
Financial counseling and hardship assistance programs including skip-a-pay and overdraft protection options
cons: Recent merger with OnPath Credit Union means Heritage Bank brand is being phased out; customers now operate under OnPath branding and governance
Limited physical branch footprint compared to larger regional or national banks, with only 4 locations listed in St. Tammany area
Website primarily redirects to OnPath Credit Union site, indicating potential loss of Heritage Bank's independent online presence and institutional identity
As a credit union entity, membership requirements and governance structures may differ from traditional banking model customers were accustomed to
No specific APR ranges, current rates, or comparative pricing information disclosed on the website to evaluate competitiveness
best_for: Southeast Louisiana residents seeking local community banking with personal relationships and service
Small business owners needing flexible business checking, loans, and merchant services within their region
Homebuyers seeking mortgage options including FHA/VA and first-time homebuyer programs with local support
Customers prioritizing community reinvestment and foundation support for local education and entrepreneurship initiatives
diagnosis: Heritage Bank of St. Tammany is best for Louisiana residents and small business owners seeking traditional community banking with local branch presence and personal service relationships. The primary caveat is that the institution has merged into OnPath Credit Union, meaning the Heritage Bank identity is transitioning and customers should expect OnPath governance, membership structures, and branding going forward rather than operating as an independent bank.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Heritage Bank of St. Tammany is a Louisiana community bank operating since 1924 with expanded branch access and digital services.
